does: This paper introduces IMAGINE, a synthesis framework that generates jailbreak-like instructions to improve LLM safety defenses. The method analyzes embedding space distributions to create adversarial examples that bridge the gap between existing safety training data and real-world attack patterns. Through iterative optimization, IMAGINE dynamically evolves text generation distributions to augment safety alignment corpora with synthesized jailbreak-like examples, addressing the critical distributional mismatch between training safety data and unseen attacks that cause LLMs to remain vulnerable despite alignment efforts.
connects: The work extends prior research on LLM safety alignment and robustness by identifying and tackling distributional shift—a fundamental challenge in adversarial machine learning. It builds on decades of work in adversarial training and data augmentation while responding directly to recent findings showing that state-of-the-art aligned models fail against out-of-distribution jailbreak attacks. The paper connects to broader literature on synthetic data generation and embedding space analysis, applying these techniques specifically to the safety domain where reactive patching cycles have proven inadequate for staying ahead of adaptive attackers.
enables: This framework opens pathways for proactive rather than reactive LLM safety development, allowing researchers to anticipate attack distributions before they emerge. The approach enables more robust alignment training by systematically exploring regions of adversarial input space, potentially scaling defenses across diverse attack modalities. Future work could extend IMAGINE to generate attacks in multimodal domains, incorporate human feedback on synthesized jailbreaks for better attack realism, or develop formal guarantees about coverage of adversarial distributions. The method also suggests opportunities for understanding and characterizing the full landscape of potential malicious prompts LLMs might encounter.
